title: Civil rights, moon landings and war wins: What Americans see as the country’s greatest achievements
summary: The NBC News poll asked American adults which historical event they thought was the country’s biggest accomplishment in its 250 years.
story: The abolition of slavery. The expansion of civil rights and voting rights. Victory in World War II. Landing people on the moon.
Limited time: Save 25% on NBC News subscription
Get exclusive reporting, live Q&As and ad-free reading.

As America nears its 250th anniversary, those are among the events its people call the greatest accomplishments in their country’s history, according to the latest NBC News poll, which was sponsored by More Perfect, a nonpartisan nonprofit group dedicated to advancing democracy.

Unlike other poll questions with multiple-choice answers, this one was completely open-ended. Still, people’s answers generally fell into three broad buckets: Close to two-thirds of respondents chose events related to the expansion of rights, while about one-sixth each chose military or diplomatic-related events and events related to science or economic achievement.

Top of mind for many was the end of slavery following the Civil War and the ratification of the 13th Amendment. The 1865 amendment formally ended slavery in the U.S., liberating more than 4 million enslaved people.
“The abolition of slavery was a magnificent achievement for us. I think one of our strengths was we used to make laws to help people,” a Texas man in his 30s told the pollsters.
Others said the 13th Amendment laid a foundation for equal opportunity to succeed in American society.

“It was a great thing because it gave freedom for everyone. Everybody has equal rights,” said a woman in her 50s. “Everybody [has] the same chance to achieve and think and speak your opinion. You have a right to speak, to work. You have a right to education, everything.”

Some respondents pointed to subsequent developments that expanded rights further for Black Americans, as well as the national expansion of voting rights to women with the passage of the 19th Amendment in 1920 — a historic marker for the women’s suffrage movement after decades of activism.

“When the women got the right to vote,” an older California woman said when she was asked to name America’s top achievement. She went on to lay out the progress she has seen over her lifetime: “There’s more opportunity to further people’s education than there used to be. I am an educator. I can see the difference from when I started in the ’70s. There’s more money for scholarships, more opportunity to get a good education.”
Title IX in 1972 prohibited gender-based discrimination in any institutions that receive federal funding.

Others pointed to American successes on the world stage, through either military or diplomatic means. They include a chunk of respondents who chose the U.S. victory in World War II alongside the Allies.
A Washington state man in his mid-60s who identifies as a Republican said the war “preserved our values — it changed the direction of the world, and it helped preserve our Western values.”

“If we had lost, freedom would have been lost. You look at what was being offered by Hitler and Mussolini and all of them — it’s the opposite of that. It’s being dictated to by a dictator,” he said. “It’s the essence of freedom that was won. You kind of just go back to democracy won over fascism.”

To another respondent, victory in World War II hit differently now given frustration with the current direction of the country. A Virginia man in his 40s, a Democrat, said the end of World War II was “when the nation was unified and most together” and when “patriotism meant something.”

“The United States is a joke in the eyes of the world right now because of our current leader,” he said, going on to criticize President Donald Trump as “narcissistic” and calling him an “utter disgrace.”
To some, like a Tennessee man in his early 30s, winning World War II was a declaration of America’s place on the world stage and its military might.

“Back-to-back being World War champs. We set the standing that the U.S. is the big daddy of the world and you shouldn’t mess with us,” he said, before he quoted former Army Staff Sgt. David Bellavia, a Medal of Honor recipient, during a speech he gave in 2019.
“So help me God, somebody else will raise your sons and your daughters,” the poll respondent said, paraphrasing Bellavia’s warning to anyone who chose war against the U.S.
Others were more swayed by technological advancements like the space program or the Industrial Revolution.

As NASA moves forward with its plans to return to the moon with the Artemis program, a number of respondents chose either the moon landing or space exploration more generally as the top achievement of America’s 250 years.
For some, the weight of the scientific accomplishment made the space program rise above the rest.

“Probably putting a man on the moon. The Apollo missions. I mean, technology at the time wasn’t exactly very good. I don’t know how they did it, but they put somebody up there. It’s really kind of amazing they were able to do that with 1960s technology,” a mid-20s man from Idaho said.
And to others, the moon landing is a reminder of what American ingenuity can achieve across many different fields.

“The U.S. is a country of remarkable technological and democratic achievement,” a Massachusetts man in his 40s said. “I think that we marked both our status as one of the world’s greatest and most stable democracies and the fruits of technological advancement that it has wrought.”

title: Inflation report could fuel concerns about higher interest rates, even as oil prices fall
summary: A closely watched inflation report is set to reveal how much price growth picked up in May — as many Americans remain mired in an affordability crunch.
story: A closely watched inflation report is set to reveal how much price growth picked up in May — and whether many American consumers remain mired in an affordability crunch.
Limited time: Save 25% on NBC News subscription
Get exclusive reporting, live Q&As and ad-free reading.
Wall Street forecasters expected the pace of personal consumption expenditures (PCE) to have quickened compared with April data amid higher oil prices and stronger consumer spending.

The monthly PCE report is the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge. New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh has said the central bank is committed to bringing inflation back to its 2% target — a level it has failed to reach for the past five years. Wall Street now anticipates the Fed will raise its key interest rate at least once by year’s end in a bid to counteract the stronger price growth.
By making it more expensive to borrow money, the Fed slows overall economic activity and, with it, the pace of price increases.

“Thursday’s PCE is set to take on greater importance for markets, especially since Federal Reserve Chair Warsh was emphatic in last week’s meeting about the central bank’s desire to achieve price stability, and this PCE reading could affect the market’s rate hike expectations,” Rick Gardner, chief investment officer at North Carolina-based RGA Investments, said in a statement.
Until Wednesday, the direction of key interest rates had begun to decouple from that of oil prices, an unusual setup given they tend to rise together as dual threats of worsening inflation.

But things got back on track Wednesday, as oil prices hit new postwar lows and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note, seen as a benchmark for borrowing costs throughout the economy, fell 9 basis points, to a level last seen in April.
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent expressed confidence that inflation is moving in the right direction.

“Now that we are, I believe, on the other side of this conflict, gas prices will come back down, inflation will come back to target,” he said Wednesday after an appearance at the Economic Club of New York.
With the war drawing to a close and vessels moving again through the Strait of Hormuz, some analysts predict Warsh will try to pursue a more “dovish” tack, or one designed to lower interest rates.

President Donald Trump remains dead set on bringing rates down, and in the run-up to his appointment, Warsh expressed some skepticism about traditional inflation gauges that would support a case for keeping rates elevated.
In a note to clients, research analysts with Citigroup said Warsh’s decision to create “task forces” to re-examine the Fed’s approaches could result in decisions that lean dovish.
“The market seems to underappreciate the flexibility implicit in appointing task forces to consider the drivers and measurement of inflation,” the analysts wrote.
Stock gains themselves have also shown signs of stuttering after a nearly unprecedented run of gains in April and May.

Over those two months, thanks largely to gains in tech and despite the Iran war, the S&P 500 rallied 16%, which has occurred only one other time outside of post-recession bounces. The other time was just a few months before Black Monday’s 1987 21% plunge.
The S&P 500 is down about 4% from its all-time high this month. The Fed’s telegraphing higher rates did not help — but the market may also simply have run out of room to run.

“Given we’d seen such a big rally that had stretched traditional valuation metrics, there simply wasn’t much space to rally further to start with,” Henry Allen, macro strategist at Deutsche Bank financial group, said in a note to clients this week.

title: Lawmakers expected to press billionaire Leon Black about Epstein ties
summary: The phrase “Please call Leon Black” appears more than 300 times in the investigative files released by the Justice Department involving Epstein, a convicted sex offender.
story: WASHINGTON — “Please call Leon Black.”
Limited time: Save 25% on NBC News subscription
Get exclusive reporting, live Q&As and ad-free reading.

That phrase appears more than 300 times in the Jeffrey Epstein investigative files released by the Justice Department, some sent in emails to Epstein by his personal assistant. Black, for years a powerful billionaire Wall Street player, and Epstein had a business relationship stretching back decades.

“Leon, as you are well aware, there is little I won’t do for you or at least try to do as a friend,” Epstein wrote in one email to Black in 2014. “And a great deal that I have already done (both known and some things that will need to remain unknown.)”

Lawmakers are expected to press Black on Friday when he testifies before the House Oversight Committee investigating the federal government’s handling of the Epstein case. They are hoping to uncover more about the financial web Epstein wove that funded his sex trafficking operations and have said they believe Black may have played a role.

The former CEO of Apollo Global Management is the latest in a line of America’s powerful ruling class, including Bill and Hillary Clinton and Bill Gates, who have gone before Congress to discuss their ties to the man who preyed on women for years.
Black’s attorney, Susan Estrich, said in a statement to NBC News that Black had called for an independent investigation of his relationship with Epstein.

Estrich said a former federal prosecutor reviewed more than 60,000 documents and interviewed more than 20 people, including Black, as part of that investigation at the behest of Apollo and found that Black had only paid Epstein for tax and real estate planning advice for his family office.

“The investigation further found that Epstein’s work had been vetted and approved by best-in-class law and accounting firms,” Estrich said in the statement. “It also found that he had no awareness of the criminal activities that led to Epstein’s arrest in 2019.”
Black has denied any wrongdoing or knowledge of Epstein’s criminal conduct. He has said he deeply regrets “having had any involvement with him.”
“With the benefit of hindsight, working with him was a horrible mistake on my part,” he said in a statement when he stepped down from Apollo.

Black’s reputation has already been tarnished by his longtime association with the wealthy sex offender. He was forced out of the company he co-founded in 2021, after Apollo revealed Black had paid Epstein hundreds of millions for financial advice from 2012 through 2017, arguing he did so despite knowing that Epstein had pleaded guilty to soliciting prostitution from an underage girl in Florida in 2008.

Yet since the document dump of millions of files, questions have only grown about Black’s financial dealings with Epstein, who died by suicide in his cell in 2019 as he awaited a criminal trial in New York. His accomplice Ghislaine Maxwell was convicted in 2021 of federal sex trafficking charges.

The release of the Epstein files has prompted multiple investigations overseas as famous people and well-connected leaders around the globe were found to have associated with him. In the U.S., only New Mexico has launched a probe into the dealings on his secluded Zorro Ranch.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

The political ramifications have been more swift; Republicans who pushed for the release of the files have seen their careers upended by President Donald Trump, who opposed the decision to make the trove public. Congress passed a law to force the release of the files.

Black and Epstein were introduced by a mutual friend in the 1990s. Epstein served as a director of Leon Black’s family foundation and later served as his financial adviser, reaping millions over the years.

Black’s phone number appears more than 200 times in the 3.5 million public files, and there are more than 100 instances of the phrase “Leon Black returned your call.” Many were sent by Epstein’s former assistant Lesley Groff.
In questioning by lawmakers on June 9, Groff was asked about Black and Epstein’s relationship.
“I thought they had a business relationship. I thought he was one of Epstein’s clients,” she said, adding that Black was a frequent visitor of Epstein’s.

Black and Epstein’s financial dealings had an alleged dark side. Black agreed to pay $62.5 million to the U.S. Virgin Islands in 2023 in order to be released from any potential action arising from a three-year investigation into an Epstein sex trafficking operation, according to the settlement.

Black was to be deposed in a lawsuit brought against Bank of America by survivors of Epstein’s abuse who argued the financial institution turned a blind eye to suspicious transfers Black made to Epstein from his Bank of America account, including $170 million for “tax and estate planning advice.”

The money, the plaintiffs alleged, was used to fund Epstein’s sex trafficking enterprise. Black said that wasn’t true. Bank of America agreed to pay $72.5 million in March without admitting any wrongdoing, and Black never testified.

Sen. Ron Wyden, D-Ore., part of the Senate Finance Committee, investigated Black for years and alleged Black may have funneled hush money payments to women, using Epstein as a middleman. Wyden said that in one email contained in the DOJ files, Epstein gave the location of a woman on Black’s payroll to a Russian government operative, and asked for suggestions on how to deal with her because she was attempting to blackmail “a group of powerful biznessman in New York.”
“Your relationship and business dealings with Jeffrey Epstein should be properly investigated by the federal government,” Wyden wrote in a letter to Black earlier this year.

Black, too, has been accused of sexual assault, claims he denies. One woman said in a lawsuit that Black had raped her at Epstein’s Manhattan townhouse in 2002. She eventually dropped the lawsuit and Black said he never met her. Another woman, a former model, accused Black of harassing and abusing her and argued he introduced her to Epstein. He countersued, and her lawsuit was dismissed.

One case remains open. Black was sued in 2023 by a woman who accused him of sexually abusing her when she was a teenager. A federal judge in April sanctioned the accuser, known as “Jane Doe,” her former attorney and the prominent law firm that had represented her for falsifying and potentially destroying evidence in the case. But the judge said the case could proceed.

Black’s attorney said in a statement after the ruling that the case never should have been filed. Estrich also said the firm Wigdor LLP had been warned the claims were fraudulent, but “Wigdor nevertheless continued the lawsuit and launched a media campaign to try to destroy Mr. Black in an attempt to shake him down for a financial windfall. Mr. Black refused because he knew the claims were false.”

Firm founder Doug Wigdor said in a statement that the firm was upset about the sanctions but pleased that its former client would get her day in court. Wigdor’s firm filed a separate claim in March accusing Black of targeting it for representing accusers; Black denies it. That lawsuit is ongoing.

title: How a Duck Named Merlin Became the Face of Team Mexico During the World Cup
summary: A duck wearing a Mexican jersey has become viral during the World Cup. Here is how he become the unofficial mascot for Team Mexico.
story: While the World Cup has brought the biggest names in soccer back into the international spotlight, one particular feathered fan is drawing just as many headlines.
Merlin, a 2-year-old duck, has taken the internet by storm, captivating fans with his tiny form-fitting Team Mexico jersey and confident waddle around his home of Mexico City.

The duck — who skyrocketed to internet fame in early June after he was seen reveling in the festivities following Mexico’s first victory in the tournament — has since become a proud symbol of the team, overshadowing FIFA’s official Mexico mascot, Zayu the Jaguar.

Merlin’s newfound celebrity status has taken him to new heights, landing him a visit with Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum. As Team Mexico’s third match of the tournament is slated for June 24, Merlin fever is likely to spread.
“I could have never imagined the magnitude of where my baby would get to,” owner Karla Gomez told NBC News. “To me, he is my son.”
How Did Merlin Become Famous?

Merlin, who has long been a fixture on the streets of Mexico City, first got a taste of fame after Team Mexico’s 2-0 victory against South Africa, the opening match of the World Cup, on June 11. Wearing not only the Mexico jersey, but also a pair of tiny socks, Merlin was captured on video, waddling around the bustling Reforma Avenue as fans celebrated the win.
Social media users, charmed by his Team Mexico spirit, began sharing memes across the internet, often pairing them with the phrase “Viva El Pato,” which translates to “Long Live The Duck!”
Some businesses have even capitalized off of the Merlin craze, with one store all the way in Canada modeling their jerseys on a row of display ducks.
Who Are Merlin’s Owners?
Gomez, Merlin’s owner, is a street beverage vendor in Mexico City. She previously told Reuters that the day Merlin went viral was like any other day for the family.
“We thought we were ​passing by unnoticed, because obviously we never thought Merlin would ‌have that boom,” she told the outlet.

The duck was initially given to Gomez as a gift by a customer two years ago, she said. The family decided to name the feathered friend Merlin after the mythical wizard because “since Merlin came into our lives, there have been many awesome changes in our world,” she told NBC News.
Merlin isn’t just a pet. He’s also an integral part of the family business, “inspecting” their work and even assuming the unofficial title of “boss,” according to the Agence France-Presse.

Merlin, who’s named after the mythical wizard, lives off a diet of fruit, veggies, crickets and fish, his owner told the AFP. Every so often, Gomez told the outlet, Merlin gets a celebratory taco too.
“He’s part of the family,” Gomez’s 14-year-old son Cristian Gomez told the AFP.
What Has Merlin Been up to Since He First Went Viral?

It seems everyone wants a piece of Merlin these days. Shortly after he climbed to superstardom, Merlin was named an official ambassador for his hometown of Mexico City, a host city for the Cup. He’s appeared on television programs and even joined Sheinbaum at her morning press conference on June 22.

“Today we brought the family that has Merlin the duck as their pet, because he has been a symbol of the World Cup, a symbol of ​what Mexican families ​stand for, of ⁠who we are as Mexican families, and that is above all else; it is what the world is seeing from ​Mexico today,” Sheinbaum said during the conference, according to Reuters.
Now, we’re just hoping that Merlin gets induckted into the International Football Hall of Fame. He deserves it.

title: Everyone’s Shipping Keke Palmer and Sean Evans. But Are They Actually Dating?
summary: Fans want Keke Palmer and Sean Evans to date. The two have been going viral for their flirty interactions with each other for years.
story: Keke Palmer and Sean Evans have been going viral for their flirty interactions with each other for years. But are the two actually dating?

The actor and the “Hot Ones” host were first linked together in November 2023 after Evans revealed during an appearance on Amelia Dimoldenberg’s “Chicken Shop Date” that Palmer was the one guest he has had a crush on. Palmer previously stopped by “Hot Ones” in 2017 and 2021.
Evans gushed about Palmer to Dimoldenberg, describing “The Burbs” star as a “very charming woman.”

Since then, Palmer appeared as a guest on “Hot Ones” again in September 2025, where the two shared a kiss. He, in turn, was interviewed by the actor on the May 26 episode of her “Baby, This is Keke Palmer” podcast, leading to comments about their undeniable chemistry on social media. A few weeks later, they were photographed getting dinner together.
While sitting on the panel for Spotify’s Conversations That Channel Culture talk at Cannes Lions on June 23, Palmer finally addressed their rumored romance.
Podcast host Jay Shetty asked Palmer if she was “serious about Sean Evans,” People reported.
Palmer replied, “We hanging out, you know what I mean?”
She added, “First of all, I love Sean Evans — I love Sean, hey Sean. That’s my guy... You never know what can arise from there. We just take it day by day. One wing at a time, so to speak.”
How did the two get to this point? Read a recap of the flirtatious moments they have shared over nearly a decade, below.
Keke Palmer and Sean Evans Have Chemistry on ‘Hot Ones’
In 2017, Palmer and Evans shared many laughs and banter when she was a guest for the first time on “Hot Ones.”

During their chat, Palmer described what type of man she is attracted to. She said men need to be more “thirsty” for women. “I need you to be thirsty, honey. Thirsty like I’m about to be after these hot wings,” she quipped.
Fans in the comments were already thinking the two would be a good match.
“They were on a date but the gag is they used the show to cover it up,” one comment read.
Another agreed, writing, “I felt like I just watched a first date.”
A third added, “Why did Sean and Keke vibe so hard? I’m laughing she brought out his personality.”
When Palmer returned in 2021, the playful dynamic between them remained.
As Evans introduced the “Scoville Level” wings he said, “Keke, because I’m your friend…”
She chimed in and joked, “Don’t act like I can’t handle myself in front of all these people, Sean.”
Once again, the people in the comments section rooted for the too.

“Here after Sean said he had a crush on Keke and I agree, she is charming,” one person wrote, while another added, “We all finally have the confirmation of sean’s crush on Keke 6yrs later. We been knowing from 1st interview and this one. Chemistry was off the charts!”
Sean Evans Reveals ‘Crush’ on Keke Palmer
Evans confirmed in November 2023 that the sparks between him and Palmer weren’t just something the internet had imagined.
While on Amelia Dimoldenberg’s “Chicken Shop Date,” he was asked to name the one guest he had a crush on.
After he had Dimoldenberg guess a few names, he shared his answer.
“Keke Palmer,” he said. “Very charming woman.”
Keke Palmer and Sean Evans Share Kiss
Palmer brought up the crush when she appeared on “Hot Ones” for a third time in September 2025.
Before she mentioned his comment, she complimented him on his physique while they were eating wings.
“Your body looks so nice and trim. I feel like for you to have this kind of gig, I don’t know how you do it,” she said. “You look great.”
He quipped, “You just have to run away from the wings.”
Palmer waited until the last moments of the interview to discuss the crush.

“There’s one more thing I’d like to say, Sean, because I saw an interview where you said I was your favorite guest and, you know, all this really sweet stuff,” she said. “And I know we’ve just had some wings but I just thought maybe we could just have a quick smooch to see if there’s a spark.”
Evans replied, “Yeah, let’s do it. Let’s do it.”
The two then ran toward each other and shared a slow-motion kiss.
“The sparks are sparking!” she said after their smooch.
He told her, “When I’m dying and my life flashes before my eyes, I’ll have that snapshot.”
Keke Palmer and Sean Evans Reminisce About Their Sweet Moments
Palmer and Evans chatted about their many sit-downs when he appeared on the “Baby, This is Keke Palmer” podcast in May.
At the end of the interview, they played Twister and sat on the floor to answer questions from Keke’s mom, Sharon Palmer, off camera.
Her mom mentioned that Evans grew up in Crystal Lake, Illinois, and that Palmer comes from Robbins, Illinois.
“When I was growing up, a kid from Crystal Lake wouldn’t have been seen with a kid from Robbins,” Palmer’s mom noted.
Keke Palmer quickly replied, “D---, is Crystal Lake where the rich folks are” before she and Evans burst into laughter. She placed her hand on his knee and she touched his back.
“I got to take my a-- to Crystal Lake. It sounds nice over there!” Keke Palmer said.
Evans sweetly responded, “Any time you want!”
Keke Palmer then said, “Shout out to Mr. and Mrs. Evans,” referring to the host’s parents.
But he misinterpreted her and smoothly said, “Oh, I thought you were talking about us.”
The next day, Palmer also shared an adorable video of her and Evans watching clips from their past interviews on TikTok.

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Power Rankings: Casa Amor’s Winners, Losers and Surprises
summary: Read TODAY.com’s Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin’s thoughts about Casa Amor week.
story: In TODAY.com’s new weekly “Love Island USA” column, content creators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin break down the latest group of Season 8 episodes, sharing their thoughts on the new bombshells, couplings and dumping. Check back here over the next six weeks to read the duo’s hot takes and follow @tessdhiggins and @sallydarrgriffin on their socials to keep up with them throughout the week.
Casa Amor has arrived and turned Season 8 of “Love Island USA” upside down.

In Episode 17, the islanders thought they would all compete in the classic “Hearts on Fire” challenge. But in a shocking new twist, instead the boys received a text informing them that they needed to leave the villa and head to Casa Amor. Minutes after meeting six new bombshells, the boys were already exploring new connections and seemingly forgetting about their couples in the main villa.
Although the women stressed about their relationships for the night, host Ariana Madix arrived in the morning and introduced 12 male bombshells.

In just a few episodes, the islanders swiftly formed connections, with some already deciding to leave their old couple in the past. For a refresher, these were the current couples before they split at the start of Casa Amor:
Trinity and Bryce
Trinity and Bryce
Melanie and Sincere
Melanie and Sincere
Kenzie and Corbin
Kenzie and Corbin
Aniya and KC
Aniya and KC
Kayda and Zach
Kayda and Zach
Singles:
Caleb
Caleb
Jen
Jen

Based on the last few episodes, TODAY.com’s Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin agree that only one couple looks poised to survive Casa Amor unscathed: Trinity and Bryce. They also are both fans of one Casa bombshell: Carl.

Tess points out that Carl already seems like a better fit for Aniya compared to KC. “Obviously, we still have to get to know him more, but I am very much looking forward to seeing her hopefully bring Carl back into the villa,” she says.
Sally Darr gushes, “I love Carl. He’s getting to know Aniya, and I think they’re a great match. Aniya needs a really sweet, attentive boy who is all about her. He has only green flags.”
Read on for Tess and Sally Darr’s thoughts about where all the couples stand, including which islanders have rubbed them the wrong way with their comments.
Best Moment

Tess: “The best moment had to be the way that they revealed that Casa Amor was happening. Having the girls expect to do the heart rate challenge, and then totally have this role reversal of the guys getting to experience that with the new Casa girls was so dynamic and compelling. It also primed them really well to actually want to participate in Casa, because so often the girls just kind of twiddle their thumbs during Casa, waiting for their guys to come back. But seeing how they all acted during the heart rate challenge helps them feel like, ‘Oh wait, I can get in on this too.’ And it gave them all the ick in such a visceral way that they were ready to step away from their couple, at least for a little bit, and truly give Casa a try.”

Sally Darr: “The best moment was hands down Trinity and Bryce both talking about how much they miss each other. Trinity was really vulnerable and talked about her fears of Bryce coupling up with someone new. We also saw Bryce talk about how all the Casa girls are great, but they just aren’t Trinity. If they stick together, they are definitely winning ‘Love Island’ and getting that 100k.”
Biggest Surprise

Tess: “The biggest surprise was that they brought in 12 guys for Casa Amor for all of the women to get to know. I thought that was genius, because it made it feel like they really got options of plenty of guys to get to know. And instead of just being assigned a random group of guys, they feel like they had a hand in picking who got to be there. Perhaps that could lead them to a pool of guys that they are more interested in, because there was some choice there. It also helps the guys feel a little more insecure, because they had the boat come by so that they could see that there were 12 tall guys on that boat. They know they only got six women. So they can see that the women are having a different Casa Amor experience than them.”

Sally Darr: “The biggest surprise was Corbin flat out saying that the Casa girls are better than the villa girls. And KC saying that the girls in the villa are just giving the new guys what they didn’t get. Both of those things felt really unnecessary, really mean, and just a weird way to speak about women, in my opinion.”
Biggest Winner

Tess: “The biggest winner for me is still Trinity and Bryce. I think that their story of mutual yearning for each other in Casa is so sweet to watch, but at the same time also very tense as the viewer, because we know that they are both yearning for each other and not really exploring Casa. But they don’t know that. But I think if they come back from Casa and lock back in and they get to hear how kind of loyal they were to each other during Casa and thinking about each other the whole time, that could set them up to go all the way to the finale and probably win.”
Sally Darr: “Trinity and Bryce. They’re sad right now, and unsure of what the other person is doing. But the public can see how they feel about each other. They are my ‘Love Island’ winners.”
Casa Amor Bombshell to Watch

Tess: “My male bombshell to watch is Carl, the one who’s currently connecting with Aniya, because I think their connection is really sweet so far. He seems very calm and secure, and could really be a rock for her throughout the rest of the game.

The female bombshell that’s sticking out to me is Parmida, because it seemed like almost all of the guys were into her. Like, she kind of had her pick of the litter. She probably could have gone with any of them, except Bryce. I think that Corbin is definitely bringing her back to the villa. I find the whole story about how they had DMed each other before was super random and kind of interesting. Because how does that even work out? Like, is that just a coincidence, or do these producers go through each other’s following list and see if there are any connections, hoping something like this happens? In any case, I think that she is definitely going to be brought back to the villa.”

Sally Darr: “I love Carl. He’s getting to know Aniya, and I think they’re a great match. Aniya needs a really sweet, attentive boy who is all about her. He has only green flags. I hope Aniya brings him back, and we see their connection blossom.”
The Couple in Jeopardy

Tess: “The couple most in jeopardy is definitely KC and Aniya. He seems so ready to bring someone else back, and I think he’s just talking in a very dismissive way about Aniya, at least what we’re being shown. And while Aniya still misses him, I think she’s definitely coming into Casa Amor with more of an intention to get to know someone like Carl, who I think seems great so far. To me, KC kind of comes off like more of a game player or someone who kind of needs constant reassurance or ego stroking. I think that Aniya might be better suited for someone who gives a bit of a calmer, more secure vibe, like Carl does. Obviously, we still have to get to know him more, but I am very much looking forward to seeing her hopefully bring Carl back into the villa.”

Sally Darr: “There are a few couples in jeopardy, but I think Melanie and Sincere are in the most trouble because they seem more unsure that they should stick with their current connection or move on to a new one. The other couples in jeopardy seem pretty sure about both selecting a new connection to explore once Casa is over. I think Melanie and Sincere have the highest chance of not being on the same page. One might choose to stay in the current connection, and the other might take a new connection.”

title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Was a Sensation. Now Queer Hockey Leagues Are Booming
summary: “Heated Rivalry” has helped key a wave of interest in hockey, with queer leagues enjoying a surge in popularity. Joe Freyer reports on the trend for TODAY.
story: “Heated Rivalry” has hockey rinks heating up.
The smash HBO Max hit, about the romance between two rival professional hockey players, has helped key a wave of interest in the sport, with queer leagues enjoying a surge in popularity.
The show, which also debuted last November on the Canadian streamer Crave, has helped grow leagues like the New York City Pride Hockey Alliance, which features 400 players who’ve taken to the ice.
“I think having a space that is for the queer community — for joy, for giggles, for kindness — is so essential,” Ian McHale, who plays with the group, told TODAY in a story that aired June 24.
McHale was spurred on, in part, by “Heated Rivalry,” which has turned stars Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie into, well, stars.
“One other reason I wanted to join hockey is because I feel like it’s hot. Hockey’s just hot, it’s fun. And ‘Heated Rivalry’ just confirmed that,” McHale said.
The show, which has been renewed for a second season, connected with fans, with some deciding to lace up skates themselves, driving an increase in players suiting up for games.

“We had a scrimmage a week ago, I think, and I didn’t know anybody here,” team captain Kate McNicholas told TODAY. “I didn’t recognize a single person, and they were like, ‘Oh yeah, they’re all new players.’ And I was like, ‘What?’”

The Big Apple isn’t the only city to see a jump in players, either. Chicago Pride Hockey, which plans to start shooting a reality series soon after being approached by four production companies, has ballooned from two teams to five, with the potential for a sixth in the fall. “Heated Rivalry” is a central reason why.
“I think that was the final pushing factor — in motivating me, yes,” newcomer Mario Calero told TODAY.

Calero also said playing hockey has helped him and others become more confident. “It just permeates not only in our locker room amongst Chicago Pride Hockey, but into everybody’s lives that we’re able to interact with,” he said.

Hockey is indeed having a moment. “Off Campus” on Prime Video is another hockey romance taking off this summer, and Netflix also just greenlit another called “Icebreaker.” Plus, the 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs were the most-watched in NHL history and year-over-year viewership among women jumped 63%.
Five years ago, Luke Prokop was 19 when he became the first pro hockey player to come out. A defenseman in the minor leagues, Prokop said other players rallied to his side.

“It’s been love, support, curiosity,” he told TODAY. “They’re asking questions. They want to know if I have a boyfriend, if he’s coming to team parties. It’s been awesome, and I think the NHL is ready for it, too.”
As for the queer community embracing the game, players like McHale continue to enjoy the experience. He sent a video of him scoring his first goal to his mother.
“My mom was like, ‘I feel so proud,’ and, like, ‘It looks like little kid Ian having so much fun.’ And I’m like, ‘That’s me.’ I feel like my little kid is shining when I’m on the ice,” he said.

title: Read With Jenna’s July 2026 Pick Is a ‘Delicious Summer Read’
summary: Jenna Bush Hager reveals her July 2026 book title, which is "Shampoo Effect" by Jenny Jackson.
story: For her July 2026 Read With Jenna pick, Jenna Bush Hager picked a book that you’ll want to pop in your tote for all of your summer adventures.
“‘The Shampoo Effect’ by Jenny Jackson is a delicious summer read. You will read it at the beach or in the park, and it will fill you with old memories,” Jenna tells TODAY.com.

The book opens on Caroline Lash, the daughter of a famous writer, who has booked a retreat to Massachusetts to try and write her own book. There, she meets Van, who is “surrounded by his childhood friends,” Jenna says — and his friends cause hurdles for their budding romance.
"The Shampoo Effect" by Jenny Jackson
“When Caroline faces an unexpected life shift, old secrets are brought to the surface and loyalties are tested,” she says.

“This is a novel about the people who have known us our whole lives and the ties that really bind us. It made me think of my own oldest friendships. I know you will love this one just as much as I do.”

Jackson, speaking to TODAY.com, wanted to follow her debut hit “Pineapple Street” with a book set in a totally different world. “Pineapple Street” followed a wealthy Brooklyn family; this, meanwhile, is about hometown friends from coastal Massachusetts.
“I couldn’t get myself comfortable with the idea that all these people felt like they knew me as this like person enmeshed in the world of ‘one perecenters’ in New York,” Jackson says.
Jackson says she’s “not a one percenter,” and she didn’t grow up in New York. But “The Shampoo Effect” was, in fact, a return to her roots.
“Part of writing ‘The Shampoo Effect’ for me was subconsciously a corrective to write about my hometown and where I’m from, and what that world growing up in Massachusetts was like,” she says.
The book is based on the town she grew up in, Ipswich, though she decided to rename it to give her creative flexibility.
Whereas “Pineapple Street” was about siblings, “The Shampoo Effect” follows a friend group in their mid-30s.

“I wanted to write about friends, because I think that one of the scariest things is that is realizing that your friendships are conditional. If you’re lucky and you come from a loving family, like I do, your family is going to love you no matter what. Those are unconditional relationships,” she says.
The characters in “The Shampoo Effect” are extremely close but their relationships are tested throughout, to the point that they might lose the friendships.

“In some ways it’s like a heightened risk with friends because these are relationships that could break. A lot of us have people who we no longer speak to from our childhood, and it’s heartbreaking,” she says.
Jackson, in addition to being a bestselling author, is also the editorial director of fiction at Knopf and has worked as an editor with Gabrielle Zevin, Emily St. John Mandel, Kevin Kwan and more.
When she’s writing, she hears her own editorial advice in her head.

“I definitely take so much of the advice that I give that I give to my writers. One of the pieces of advice that I give some of my favorite novelists is to not fall in love with your research in the novel,” she says.

And she also tries to be the voice of reason. “There are moments where I’m about to do something slightly crazy, and I’m like, what would you tell your writer? To log off social media and to not read the rest of this review,” she says.

While reading about Caroline, Van, Bailey, Augusta, Fran and the other friends of the book, she wants people to ask themselves about their own old friendships, “and the moment when you and your old friends start to make different life choices.”

title: Mariska Hargitay Says Broadway Debut Was ‘Cripplingly Terrifying.’ She Hung These 4 Pictures in Her Dressing Room for Inspiration
summary: Mariska Hargitay talks to Hoda Kotb about Broadway Debut in ‘Every Brilliant Thing” and the pictures of the people that inspire her: Jalen Brunson, Lindsey Vonn.
story: After 27 seasons as Oliva Benson on The “Law & Order: Special Victims Unit,” you would think no acting gig could give Mariska Hargitay stage fright. But she says her Broadway debut in “Every Brilliant Thing” did just that.

Stepping on the Broadway stage “was terrifying… cripplingly so. It really came and grabbed me, and I had no idea what was happening,” she tells Hoda Kotb in the June 24 episode of the “Making Space” podcast, which just returned for its latest season.

“I am comfortable at SVU. Are there days that are very challenging and scenes that are challenging. Yes. Am I always trying to get better? Yes. But I have been doing it for a long time, and there is a certain amount of expertise,” she says. “With this play, there wasn’t that expertise. I’m a novice, and I’m going into this new. There was nothing familiar about this.”

Saying no to the opportunity was never an option. She says the script felt like “it fell out of the sky from God.” She describes the play as “beautiful, emotional, poignant, hilarious, meaningful, and life-affirming.”
“I just couldn’t believe my good fortune, and to go in after Daniel Radcliffe, it was an immediate yes.”

Not only is Hargitay stepping onto the Broadway stage for the first time, but she’s doing so in a solo show. In “Every Brilliant Thing,” a single actor narrates the storyline, which features audience participation and explores depression, suicide, and the small joys that make life worth living.
“You are doing something for the very first time at age 62, so that gets claps,” cheers Kotb before asking what it’s like to do something like that for the very first time.
Hargitay says people warned her about the physical and mental feat she was about to conquer — but at first, she didn’t believe them.
“Everyone’s like, ‘You’re gonna have to get your energy up.’ And I was like, sweetheart, I’ve been on SVU for like 89 years. My energy is good, I got this,” she jokes.

But once rehearsals started, she says she quickly realized what a huge undertaking the project was. Going from the top of her game to being a complete novice left her needing a nap by 3 p.m. every day because her “brain was processing so much.”
Hargitay adds that she couldn’t sleep for three days before opening night, because of the “terror running through her.”
“It was so difficult, and everything about it was new, and yet it was such an incredible thing” she says. “Talk about ‘Feel the fear and do it anyway.’”
To help her find the courage to step on stage every night, she has four pictures hanging in her dressing room: Jalen Brunson, Billie Jean King, Lindsey Vonn, and the cast of Hamilton.

When she looks at the photos, “I think about what those people have taught me,” she says. “Billie Jean wrote ‘One Ball at a Time.’ Think about tennis, it’s one ball at a time — and it’s one moment at a time on stage, and if something doesn’t go right, you just go. We keep going. I think about Jalen. It’s the same thing. The more that I learn about him and how he was raised, his work ethic, his humility ... he doesn’t take in the highs, he doesn’t take the lows. He’s about the work. What can we do better?”

“I’m just admiring the fact that you’re such a student in human nature, because I think people get to a certain point and say, ‘I got this far and I seem to have done it well’ and then they end up being the ones who spend time giving advice. It seems like you’re someone who says ‘teach me, teach me,’” says Kotb before asking Harigitay what she has learned from Lindsey Vonn.
“So much: tenacity, strength, the power of getting up again, resilience. She’s my hero. The way she does it. Her attitude. The clarity Jalen and Lindsey have…”

During their sitdown, Kotb also reflects on how much of Hargitay's inspiration comes from athletes. “These are my heroes,” Mariksa responds. “I think that many athletes are my heroes. My dad was an athlete. That mentality of: We just work harder.”
It’s a lesson she now passes down to her own children. “I keep saying to my kids, ‘We can do hard things.’”
Hargitay describes doing the hard thing and standing on the other side of the fear as “euphoria.”

“Isn’t that the point of it?” she asks Hoda. “Being brave and courageous, and to feel those feelings again and not know what’s going to happen was so incredibly exciting and terrifying? Now, I can’t wait every night. I cannot wait to go on.”

title: Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup Cleats Have a Special Nod to His Family
summary: During the 2026 World Cup, Cristiano Ronaldo has his loved ones with him every step of the way.
story: During what has already been a history-making 2026 World Cup, Cristiano Ronaldo keeps his loved ones close.

The 41-year-old Portuguese soccer star scored two goals during his team’s match against Uzbekistan on June 23, becoming the first player to score in six different World Cups. His family was with him every step of the way.

That’s because Ronaldo has his five children’s names on his cleats. In close ups of his footwear, under the Nike logo, one cleat has the names Matteo and Chris Jr. and the other has Eva Maria, Alana Martina and Bella.

Ronaldo is father to 16-year-old Cristiano Jr. The athlete has never publicly shared the name of his eldest son’s mother, saying in 2015 on “The Jonathan Ross Show,” “Some points in life (are) private and people have to respect the privacy. When Cristiano (grows) up, I am always going to say the truth to him because he deserves it, because he is my son. But I am not going to say because people want me to say.”
The superstar also welcomed twins Eva Maria and Mateo in 2017 with the help of a surrogate.
Ronaldo also shares 8-year-old daughter Alana Martina and 4-year-old daughter Bella with fiancée Georgina Rodríguez.
Baby Bella was a twin. However, the couple announced their son’s death shortly after Bella’s birth.

Over the years, Ronaldo has opened up about the importance of his family and kids. In an Instagram video, he’s said that when he receives any type of accolades, “I’m always thinking about my family, especially my kids.”
He called them his motivation to continue playing the sport.
Additionally, in an ESPN interview, Ronaldo touched on how he could have millions and billions of dollars “but the most important thing is... family.”
“But in the beginning, your family it’s always with you for the good moments and for the bad moments,” he added.

title: Should You Let Your Kids Watch ‘Love Island USA’?
summary: “Love Island USA” is taking over the internet once again this summer. Experts weigh in on whether its content is ok for kids to watch.
story: “Love Island USA” is taking over the internet once again this summer, set against the backdrop of its staple luxury villa in Fiji.

In homes around the world, viewers are tuning in to watch the islanders stir up drama, test their relationships and compete in messy challenges — often involving make outs and some form of paint or condiment. But even with all the buzz, should you let your teen watch?

Kier Gaines, licensed therapist and social media parenting expert, weighs in on the June 23 show of TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle. Polly Conway, senior TV editor at Common Sense Media, which provides age-based media reviews for families, also shares guidance with TODAY.com.
Here’s what parents should consider, according to experts.
(“Love Island USA” streams on Peacock, which is owned by NBCUniversal, TODAY’s parent company.)
What to Know About the Content

Common Sense Media currently rates Love Island USA for ages 15 and up for the sex, cursing and drinking portrayed in the show, per its website. For younger teens especially, the show’s sexual content and complex relationship dynamics may be too much, Conway says.
For older teens, the key question is whether they’re mature enough to “know that it’s just entertainment,” and “has very little to do with real love and relationships,” she says.
It’s important that teens can identify what portrayals of healthy relationships and real love do and don’t look like in the media, Conway says, and “Love Island USA” can blur those lines.
The show may also contribute to unrealistic ideas around body image, Conway adds, especially because the islanders are typically portrayed in swimsuits.
Consider Maturity, Not Just Age

While many parents look for a clear-cut guide on when their kid should be allowed to start consuming certain content, it’s not always that straightforward, Gaines tells Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones.

“You have to level set and you have to know your kid,” he says. “Readiness is not commensurate with age. You have 12 year olds who are more ready for mature conversations, you have 15 year olds who are less ready.”

Much of that readiness comes down to the culture in your home, he adds, and how open you are as a parent about mature topics. He suggests asking yourself questions: Do you talk about these issues with your child openly? Are you, as a parent, comfortable with discussing mature subjects?
Conway agrees that there is no one-size-fits-all answer: “Every teen is different,” she says.
They Might Be Watching Anyway — What to Do Next
Whether a parent likes it or not, “Love Island USA” might be one of those shows where teens are watching without parental permission, Conway says, especially due to its popularity on social media.
“Once you enter into the teen years, parents do lose a bit of control, but that definitely opens up the opportunity for conversation,” she adds.
Gaines says those conversations are easiest when they’re initiated in real-time. Typically, that means trying to watch the show together.
But, he acknowledges that’s not always realistic for every family — or every show. Sometimes, “It’s just crazy uncomfortable,” he admits.

Still, that discomfort can be productive. Rather than avoiding a difficult conversation, Gaines suggests using the opportunity as a potential starting point for open dialogue. “It’s more of a gateway than an enemy.”

title: This Documentarian Followed the U.S. Men’s National Soccer Team for 4 Years. Here’s Her Biggest Takeaway
summary: “U.S. Against the World” executive producer Janina Pelayo shares her experience filming the United States Men’s National Soccer Team ahead of and during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
story: For the past four years, Janina Pelayo has traveled with the United States Men’s National Soccer Team, capturing their road to the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
The U.S. is hosting this year’s tournament along with Mexico and Canada, marking the first time three countries share the World Cup duties. Pelayo knew this moment had to be documented.

The result was “U.S. Against the World: Four Years with the Men’s National Soccer Team,” an HBO Original docuseries produced by Pelayo’s production company Park Stories. Born in the Philippines and raised in Juneau, Alaska, Pelayo tells TODAY.com she didn’t grow up watching soccer but understood its global impact.

To create the five-part sports documentary, now streaming, the executive producer led production across six countries and built friendships with the players and their families. She praised the choices they all make to help the men make their dreams come true.

“The biggest sacrifice is probably (the) time you spend away from your families,” she says. “And it just goes to show the amount of support and sacrifice that their families also undergo, not just them, the wives at home... All of their stories have pulled on my heartstrings.”
While the episodes led to the World Cup kickoff, Pelayo and her team are still filming.

“You never know where the magic might happen,” she says. “There could be an episode after the World Cup, or this could be the first episode of Season 2. Who knows. I just think documenting this moment in history as the sport emerges in the U.S. is going to be worth (it).”
She adds that as the sport becomes more popular in the U.S. and more kids decide to pursue it, “it’s meaningful to all the generations.”
“It’s a moment in time that we’ll never get. World Cups on home soil come across every, maybe, 30 years,” she says. “It’s a special time for sure.”
Below, in her own words, Pelayo shares her World Cup memories, as well as what she’s most looking forward to as the tournament is underway.

I was born in the Philippines, so I didn’t really grow up with the sport. Basketball is still the largest sport there. But my first World Cup memory was, I believe, the 2014 World Cup. I was in Seattle and it was just such an electric atmosphere of people walking about. We were (watching) a game and I didn’t know anything about the sport, the team, but I was in it. It was fun. It built community, which is kind of hard to do as a transplant. (I) had moved to different cities, so finding a common community in the bar was so much fun.
I’m really late to the party. I think it’s a great event for everyone to get into. It’s amazing. There’s so much to peel back behind soccer and behind national teams, and you just get drawn in.

Soccer is such a beautiful sport culture and it’s that healthy masculinity that we want to see and highlight. These guys are very young, they care about doing the right thing, even if it’s a hard thing to do, they’re going to do it. It’s really beautiful documenting them trying to build a legacy because they haven’t built it yet. They’re in the midst of doing it right now. That just resonates with everybody on a day-to-day level, trying to do the best they can. It’s also eye-opening for me seeing what their families go through and how they step up to the plate in order to join this mission.

There was a fan that approached us during the open public training and he basically thanked Yunus Musah for showcasing his faith. He’s a practicing Muslim on Team USA. That to me is super meaningful because everyone’s trying to compete and be on the team, and there’s a million different ways to get here. To highlight Yunus’ story, he was a huge integral part of the team and maybe he makes his way back. But just to showcase that time in their lives, and whether it’s a moment of success or a moment of hardship, it’s leaving the door open for a great comeback story.

They’re still all so young. So for me, the biggest takeaway is just, stay tuned. You never know who you might see pop back in or who might emerge as your favorite player because their stories are still being told.

title: Karamo Brown Breaks Silence On Skipping ‘Queer Eye’ Cast’s TODAY Appearance — and If He Regrets It
summary: Karamo Brown has revealed why he skipped an interview on TODAY with the rest of the “Queer Eye” cast in January ahead of the show’s final season.
story: Karamo Brown has revealed why he skipped an interview on TODAY with the rest of the “Queer Eye” cast in January ahead of the show’s final season.
Brown, 45, was supposed to join Antoni Porowski, Tan France, Jonathan Van Ness and Jeremiah Brent on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle on Jan. 20 to discuss the 10th and final season of the Netflix show.

However, that morning he released a statement through his assistant that said Brown “felt mentally and emotionally abused for years, and he has been advised by his therapist to protect himself and his peace by not attending.”
In his return to TODAY on June 23, Brown said he direct messaged  Sheinelle Jones and Jenna Bush Hager the morning of the January interview to say he would not be attending.
Jenna asked if he had any regrets about declining to be part of the group interview.

“One of the things that I’ve been teaching people right now is that two things can be true at once,” he said. “‘Queer Eye’ was one of the most amazing shows — the impact that it had culturally for 10 years and what we were able to do. One of the things I’ll say about my cast, no matter what we were going through, we always made sure that the hero was No. 1, that the person we were helping, the crew worked so hard.

“But on the flip side, you can also have a bad experience,” he continued. “And you can also feel as if you’re not being protected and things are not going well. So while I loved what we did, it didn’t mean that I loved what was happening to me.”

Brown’s comments on TODAY came three weeks after his cover story in People was published, in which he stated he clashed with some of his costars and suffered a relapse with alcohol addiction in 2018 during the third season of the show.

“We strongly disagree with any characterization that concerns raised during the production of ‘Queer Eye’ were ignored, dismissed or allowed to continue unchecked,” the show’s production company, ITV America and Scout Productions, told People in a statement.
While he missed the TODAY interview in January, he did send in a video congratulating Jenna and Sheinelle on the launch of their show and speaking about “Queer Eye.”
“I just want to say thank you to all the fans who supported us and rocked with us for 10 seasons,” he said.

“This new season is going to be amazing. You’re going to love every minute of it. And just like the themes of this season, I’m modeling what I believe is most important, which I want to remind you all: Love yourselves and protect yourselves,” he added. “That’s why I’m here, at home, and not there.”
His costars also commented during the TODAY interview about his absence.
“I think (we’re) definitely a little surprised, but at the same time, like, look, we’re so sorry that he’s not here,” Porowski said.
The Emmy-winning TV host, who has developed a new wellness app called Kē (pronounced “Key”), gave an update on TODAY Tuesday about how he is doing with his physical and mental health.

“I’m in a space right now where I’m just making sure I teach people, you don’t have to live in any way that’s going to make you feel bad, make you feel like you’re not worthy enough,” he said on TODAY. “I’m trying to teach people that you can get out of those situations.”
He said he relapsed in 2018 after 12 years of sobriety due to the stress of “Queer Eye.”
“The pressure of what I was experiencing and feeling as if like, ‘If I speak up I’m going to lose my job and my livelihood,’” he said.
He added that his addiction struggles “never got on set” and said he was “a weekend coper.” Brown said he is now sober.

“I never had once anybody on staff, crew ever say, ‘Hey your partying is getting in the way,’” he said. “I’d be honest about that if I had. But I knew for me that I wasn’t coping well. It was after a week of feeling isolated, betrayed, hurt, talked to in a certain way, I would then feel like ‘OK, I need a drink.”

title: ‘Abby Offsides’, the Latest Book from Jenna’s Imprint, Is a Rom-Com for the World Cup-Obsessed
summary: What to know about Abby Offsides by Anna McCallie, the latest book from Jenna’s imprint.
story: Anna McCallie jokes that people have been confusing her with the protagonist of her debut novel, Abby McIntyre.
“There’s a lot of me in her, so maybe it was I was working subconsciously,” McCallie tells TODAY.com.

McCallie’s debut novel, “Abby Offsides,” was published through Jenna Bush Hager’s imprint, Thousand Voices x Random House Publishing Group, and comes out on June 23. Jenna, on TODAY with Jenna and Sheinelle on June 23, described it as “Notting Hill” meets “Ted Lasso.”
“I don’t normally read rom-coms, but Henry (Hager) would be like, ‘Want to talk?’ And I’d be like, ‘Shhh!’” she said.

The romance follows an American named Abby who moves to Liverpool after her her engagement abruptly ends. She takes a job working for a Premier League football club, where she meets a Scottish star midfielder named Lachlan Ramsey.
Similarly, McCallie moved to London when she was 30 and had to completely start her life over. As for how much of McCallie and her experiences across the pond made it into Abby’s character?

“The high-level arcs of our lives are very similar. This idea of moving to a country when you don’t know anybody. You’re coming to a world where everybody already has a life. How do I build a life here? How do I make this my home?” McCallie tells TODAY.com.
For both her and Abby, that entailed “meeting the right people and building a chosen family.”
“Abby Offsides” is the fifth book McCallie started, the third she finished and the first she sold. It started as a bedtime story she told herself to try to fall asleep.

“I tried this idea of, ‘Can I just tell myself a gentle story at night?’ Imagine a woman. What is she wearing? What does her office look like? I tried to make it interesting enough that it like crowds out the other thoughts in my brain, but not so interesting that it keeps me up,” she says.
There was just one problem: “It became way too interesting.”
She was wrapped up in the story and was “completely failing” at going to sleep. Her friend, upon hearing her problem, replied, “You realize you’re writing a book, right?”
The end result combines two of McCallies loves: The U.K. and soccer. The latter fandom is thanks to a crush she had her senior year of college.

“He was a big soccer fan. I would sit in the basement of our dorm and watch games with him and try to impress. Spoiler alert: it didn’t work out between the two of us, but I realized that I actually loved this sport, and so soccer has actually been one of the longest and healthiest relationships of my life,” she says.
While reading, she hopes readers ask themselves, “Is there anything that’s holding you back? What would it look like for you to reinvent yourself?”
“I think that kind of reflection is important, because otherwise the years pass and you realize maybe you’re not being the optimal version of yourself,” she says.

title: Jeezy Says He Was ‘Worried About Pushback’ While Making His Most Personal Project
summary: Hear Craig Melvin’s discussion with Jeezy on an episode of his podcast Open Book with Craig Melvin. Jeezy said he was scared of pushback for his memoir.
story: Risk-taking has long defined Grammy-nominated rapper and author Jay “Jeezy” Jenkins’ career. But when it came time to publish his memoir, “Adversity for Sale: Ya Gotta Believe,” he admitted he wasn’t immune to doubt.
Jeezy, 48, was “worried about pushback” when he released his book in 2023, he told Craig Melvin on the June 23 episode of his “Glass Half Full” podcast.
He recalled having a vision for the memoir but raised some concerns early on.
“I was like, ‘Yo, I’m just concerned about the pushback because everybody might not see this how I saw it, so I’m going to just write this from an honest place,’” he said.
Even writing candidly from personal experience, Jeezy knew not everyone mentioned in the book or present in the moments described would share his perspective.

“I don’t got to be the hero, I just want to tell the story,” he said. “Because once you start talking about people or things or incidents and times, everybody has a different understanding of what happened.”

Really, the rapper’s journey to publishing “Adversity for Sale” wasn’t straightforward. It took years of revision and effort, including switching publishers, he told Craig. But eventually, he landed with a team that aligned on his vision.

“It’s been about defying the odds and adversity ... that’s all I had to give,” Jeezy said. “I’m not a doctor, I’m not a lawyer, I’m not a physician. I’m just telling you what I’ve been through and how I overcame it. And to me, that’s worth more than anything because I actually had to go through it.”
And Jeezy’s honesty about his experiences paid off.
“Nobody ever said anything,” he said, “Everybody that I saw was like, ‘Man, the book was incredible.’”

Plus, beyond the personal praise, Jeezy earned one of publishing’s most prestigious designations: making it on the New York Times Best Sellers list. To him, the milestone stood apart from his many music accolades.

“I was just like, ‘Wow, this goes along with the man you’re becoming,’” he said. “I sold millions of CDs when you were selling CDs, got streams, and that’s great. But it’s like, when you start thinking about things that are outside of the box or outside of who you’re known to be, when you hear New York Times bestseller besides your name, it just makes you think.”
The recognition also felt like a validation of his persistence with telling his story.

“I almost gave up on (writing the book) a few times. I stuck with it, I changed, I did whatever I had to do, but I stuck with … just my way of looking at it and executed it. And this happened,” he said.
Jeezy’s success in music, lived experience and literary achievement didn’t go unnoticed by Craig.
“There are very few people that achieve street cred and ivory tower cred,” Craig pointed out.
“Correct,” Jeezy replied.

title: Adam Sandler’s Over-the-Top Anniversary Gesture Has Left People Speechless
summary: Adam Sandler shared a skywriting message for wife Jackie in honor of their 23rd anniversary. Here’s what he did.
story: Adam Sandler just set the bar sky high for husbands everywhere.
The “Happy Gilmore” star shared a skywriting message for his wife, Jackie, in honor of their 23rd anniversary.
“23 A 🤍J” read the message, which he posted on Instagram on June 22.
“Happy 23rd anniversary to my sweetheart. Love you forever,” he captioned the post.
“❤️❤️❤️,” their daughter Sadie, 20, commented.
Other people marveled at Sandler’s gesture.
“And here I am fighting for my life to get flowers,” someone commented.
“Dang I don’t even get a card,” another person wrote.
“I want this,” someone else wrote.
“Couple goals,” commented comedian Whitney Cummings.
“Seems like yesterday…” recalled Sandler’s former “Saturday Night Live” co-star Rob Schneider.

Adam Sandler reportedly met Jackie Sandler on the set of his 1999 movie, “Big Daddy,” in which she had a small role as a waitress. They got married on June 22, 2003. Jackie Sandler has appeared in several of her husband’s films, including “Little Nicky,” “Eight Crazy Nights,” 50 First Dates,” “Grown Ups,” “Just Go With It,” both “Murder Mystery” movies and “You Are So Not Invited to My Bat Mitzvah.”
In addition to Sadie, the couple also shares daughter Sunny, 17. Both of their kids have also appeared in several of their father’s films.
In 2020, Sandler commemorated the day he and his wife met.
“22 years ago today we locked eyes and fell deep. Look forward to the next 22, young lady. Love you my forever girl,” he captioned a throwback photo of them on Instagram.
As for how he and his wife keep the magic alive, Adam Sandler says he and the missus maintain a deep connection.
“We’re very close. We like to talk, like to laugh, like to have fun, and like to think about things and take care of our kids,” he told People in November 2025.

“I’m just more thankful than I’ve ever been. Happy I got to do this life, happy all the people who’ve been with me during all this stuff, and helped me out, and (it’s) just been a phenomenal time. My whole family’s always been great to me. My wife and I talk about stuff, and what to do, and what to do next, and it’s just been a very cool life.”

title: Beyoncé Reveals the Sweet Reason Jay-Z First Got Locs, and It Involves Blue Ivy
summary: Beyoncé shares the story behind Jay-Z’s dreadlocks and why he recently made another dramatic hair transformation.
story: After switching up his look years ago in honor of his and Beyoncé’s oldest child, 14-year-old Blue Ivy Carter, Jay-Z recently made another dramatic hair transformation.

On May 30, the rap superstar surprised fans when he headlined the Roots Picnic in Philadelphia. Following years of sporting lengthy dreadlocks, Jay-Z appeared on stage rocking a full afro. In a YouTube video shared on the account for Cécred, Beyoncé’s hair care line, the singer revealed the significance behind her husband’s latest ‘do and his hair journey over about the last decade.

In between clips of Jay-Z touring Philadelphia and watching a stylist or his wife comb out his locs, Beyoncé explained in a voiceover that her husband wanted to change his hair to celebrate the legacy of his father, Adnis Reeves, because his favorite sports team was from the City of Brotherly Love.
“His dad used to rock a fro, so he wanted to rock a fro in his honor,” she shared.

The superstar added that it wasn’t the first time Jay-Z used his hair to convey a powerful message. She said that her husband had been wearing locs for over eight years to boost Blue Ivy’s confidence.
“I thought it was always beautiful that the real reason Jay grew out his hair was to show Blue that her hair was like his hair,” Beyoncé shared.
The clip then cut to footage of a young Blue Ivy with short and curly hair, smiling and posing in the “Formation” music video and other projects.

“Let me rewind and tell you some backstory. Our daughter was about five, and she wasn’t feeling very confident about her hair at that time,” Beyoncé recalled. “When she told her father that, it inspired Jay to grow out his hair.”
She continued, “We really wanted her to love and embrace every inch of her gorgeous curls.”

Beyoncé said no one predicted how long Jay-Z’s hair would grow. “Who would’ve thunk all of this healthy hair could grow beyond that low-cut Caesar with the deep waves?” she asked, referencing the lyrics to the Destiny’s Child song “Soldier.”

The “Alien Superstar” singer said that her daughter realized at 5 years old that she had the same hair texture as her father. So after eight years of growth, Beyoncé didn’t want her husband to get rid of his locs.
“I was pretty obsessed with Jay’s locks. Honestly, I didn’t really want him to cut them. It’s a lot of memories stored in locs,” she noted, comparing the style to “antennas.”
“Hair grows, people grow, so the transformation can be emotional,” she explained before the rest of the video showed the six-day removal process and an extra day of detangling that led to his afro.
“And on show day, his fro was beautiful, from start to finish,” she proudly concluded. “He is forever ‘The Blueprint.’”
She also titled the video “The Blueprint,” after the rapper’s successful and influential sixth studio album.

title: Meet the Casa Amor Bombshells for ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8
summary: “Love Island USA” Season 8 is making Casa Amor history. Learn everything you need to know about the 18 new islanders. Here’s the cast and their Instagrams.
story: “Love Island USA” Season 8 is making Casa Amor history.

During Episode 17, the moment “Love Island” fans wait for every season — the return of Casa Amor — finally arrived. But this time, the Peacock dating show introduced the bombshells with a twist. While the OG girls prepared to impress their men and raise their heart rates in the classic “Hearts on Fire” challenge, the boys received a text instructing them to sneak out of the villa and head to Casa Amor.

There, the boys participated in the “Hearts on Fire” challenge with the six new bombshells. Meanwhile, the OG girls watched all the shenanigans unfold in real time via a giant screen set up in the main villa, where they heard and saw how eager the boys were to get to know the other ladies.

All six bombshells made their mark, sharing kisses with the boys and showing off their seductive dance moves. After a few minutes, Corbin already declared that he planned to couple up with one of them.

And what about the male Casa Amor bombshells? Well, they won’t be fully revealed until Episode 18, out June 22. However, host Ariana Madix teased another surprise in the episode preview: 12 male bombshells will enter the main villa and flirt with the OG girls all week long.
